links.title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience

primary_link.title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience

papers.summary: Autonomous mobile GUI agents have attracted increasing attention along with the advancement of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). However, existing methods still suffer from inefficient learning from failed trajectories and ambiguous credit assignment under sparse rewards for long-horizon GUI tasks. To that end, we propose UI-Voyager, a novel two-stage self-evolving mobile GUI agent. In the first stage, we employ Rejection Fine-Tuning (RFT), which enables the continuous co-evolution of data and models in a fully autonomous loop. The second stage introduces Group Relative Self-Distillation (GRSD), which identifies critical fork points in group rollouts and constructs dense step-level supervision from successful trajectories to correct failed ones. Extensive experiments on AndroidWorld show that our 4B model achieves an 81.0% Pass@1 success rate, outperforming numerous recent baselines and exceeding human-level performance. Ablation and case studies further verify the effectiveness of GRSD. Our method represents a significant leap toward efficient, self-evolving, and high-performance mobile GUI automation without expensive manual data annotation.
papers.summary_zh: 隨著多模態大型語言模型 (MLLMs) 的進步，自主移動 GUI 代理受到了越來越多的關注。然而，現有方法仍然面臨從失敗軌跡中學習效率低下，以及在長期 GUI 任務中稀疏獎勵下信用分配模糊的問題。為此，我們提出了 UI-Voyager，這是一種新穎的兩階段自我演化移動 GUI 代理。在第一階段，我們採用 Rejection Fine-Tuning (RFT)，它使數據和模型能夠在完全自主的循環中持續共同演化。第二階段引入了 Group Relative Self-Distillation (GRSD)，它識別群組推演中的關鍵分叉點，並從成功軌跡中建構密集的步驟級別監督以糾正失敗的軌跡。在 AndroidWorld 上的大量實驗表明，我們的 4B 模型達到了 81.0% 的 Pass@1 成功率，優於眾多近期基準方法並超越人類水準的表現。消融實驗和案例研究進一步驗證了 GRSD 的有效性。我們的方法代表了在無需昂貴的手動數據標註下，實現高效、自我演化且高性能移動 GUI 自動化的一個重大飛躍。
papers.title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience
papers.title_zh: UI-Voyager: 一個透過失敗經驗學習的自我演化 GUI 代理

papers.summary: While proprietary systems such as Seedance-2.0 have achieved remarkable success in omni-capable video generation, open-source alternatives significantly lag behind. Most academic models remain heavily fragmented, and the few existing efforts toward unified video generation still struggle to seamlessly integrate diverse tasks within a single framework. To bridge this gap, we propose OmniWeaving, an omni-level video generation model featuring powerful multimodal composition and reasoning-informed capabilities. By leveraging a massive-scale pretraining dataset that encompasses diverse compositional and reasoning-augmented scenarios, OmniWeaving learns to temporally bind interleaved text, multi-image, and video inputs while acting as an intelligent agent to infer complex user intentions for sophisticated video creation. Furthermore, we introduce IntelligentVBench, the first comprehensive benchmark designed to rigorously assess next-level intelligent unified video generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OmniWeaving achieves SoTA performance among open-source unified models. The codes and model will be made publicly available soon. radar.summary: In Natural Language Processing (NLP), variation is typically seen as noise and "normalised away" before processing, even though it is an integral part of language. links.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?

primary_link.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?

top5.summary: Self-distillation has emerged as an effective post-training paradigm for LLMs, often improving performance while shortening reasoning traces. However, in mathematical reasoning, we find that it can reduce response length while degrading performance. We trace this degradation to the suppression of epistemic verbalization - the model's expression of uncertainty during reasoning. Through controlled experiments varying conditioning context richness and task coverage, we show that conditioning the teacher on rich information suppresses uncertainty expression, enabling rapid in-domain optimization with limited task coverage but harming OOD performance, where unseen problems benefit from expressing uncertainty and adjusting accordingly. Across Qwen3-8B, DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B, and Olmo3-7B-Instruct, we observe performance drops of up to 40%. Our findings highlight that exposing appropriate levels of uncertainty is crucial for robust reasoning and underscore the importance of optimizing reasoning behavior beyond merely reinforcing correct answer traces.
top5.summary_zh: Self-distillation 已成為 LLMs 有效的後訓練範式，通常能提高性能並縮短推理路徑。然而，在數學推理中，我們發現它可能會在縮短回應長度的同時降低性能。我們將這種性能下降歸因於對認知性語言化 (epistemic verbalization) 的抑制——即模型在推理過程中表達不確定性的方式。透過改變條件背景豐富度和任務覆蓋範圍的受控實驗，我們表明，以豐富資訊條件化 teacher 會抑制不確定性表達，這有助於在有限任務覆蓋範圍下快速進行領域內 (in-domain) 優化，但會損害 OOD (Out-of-Distribution) 性能，因為未見過的問題受益於表達不確定性並據此調整。在 Qwen3-8B、DeepSeek-Distill-Qwen-7B 和 Olmo3-7B-Instruct 上，我們觀察到性能下降高達 40%。我們的發現強調，表達適當程度的不確定性對於穩健推理至關重要，並突顯了優化推理行為的重要性，而不僅僅是強化正確答案路徑。
top5.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?
top5.title_zh: 為什麼 Self-Distillation (有時) 會降低 LLMs 的推理能力？

links.title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents

primary_link.title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents

top5.summary: Computer-use agents (CUAs) hold great promise for automating complex desktop workflows, yet progress toward general-purpose agents is bottlenecked by the scarcity of continuous, high-quality human demonstration videos. Recent work emphasizes that continuous video, not sparse screenshots, is the critical missing ingredient for scaling these agents. However, the largest existing open dataset, ScaleCUA, contains only 2 million screenshots, equating to less than 20 hours of video. To address this bottleneck, we introduce CUA-Suite, a large-scale ecosystem of expert video demonstrations and dense annotations for professional desktop computer-use agents. At its core is VideoCUA, which provides approximately 10,000 human-demonstrated tasks across 87 diverse applications with continuous 30 fps screen recordings, kinematic cursor traces, and multi-layerfed reasoning annotations, totaling approximately 55 hours and 6 million frames of expert video. Unlike sparse datasets that capture only final click coordinates, these continuous video streams preserve the full temporal dynamics of human interaction, forming a superset of information that can be losslessly transformed into the formats required by existing agent frameworks. CUA-Suite further provides two complementary resources: UI-Vision, a rigorous benchmark for evaluating grounding and planning capabilities in CUAs, and GroundCUA, a large-scale grounding dataset with 56K annotated screenshots and over 3.6 million UI element annotations. Preliminary evaluation reveals that current foundation action models struggle substantially with professional desktop applications (~60% task failure rate). Beyond evaluation, CUA-Suite's rich multimodal corpus supports emerging research directions including generalist screen parsing, continuous spatial control, video-based reward modeling, and visual world models. All data and models are publicly released.
top5.summary_zh: Computer-use agents (CUAs) 在自動化複雜的桌面工作流程方面前景廣闊，但通用型 agent 的進展卻受制於連續、高品質人類示範影片的稀缺性。最近的研究強調，連續影片而非稀疏的螢幕截圖，是擴展這些 agent 的關鍵缺失環節。然而，現有最大的開放數據集 ScaleCUA 僅包含 200 萬張螢幕截圖，相當於不到 20 小時的影片。為解決此瓶頸，我們推出了 CUA-Suite，這是一個大規模的生態系統，包含專業桌面 computer-use agents 的專家影片示範和密集標註。其核心是 VideoCUA，它提供了約 10,000 個人類示範任務，涵蓋 87 種不同應用程式，具備連續 30 fps 的螢幕錄影、運動學游標軌跡和多層次推理標註，總計約 55 小時和 600 萬幀的專家影片。與僅捕捉最終點擊座標的稀疏數據集不同，這些連續影片串流保留了人類互動的完整時間動態，形成了可無損轉換為現有 agent 框架所需格式的資訊超集。CUA-Suite 還提供了兩個互補資源：UI-Vision，一個用於評估 CUAs 接地 (grounding) 和規劃能力的嚴格基準；以及 GroundCUA，一個包含 56K 標註螢幕截圖和超過 360 萬個 UI 元素標註的大規模接地數據集。初步評估顯示，當前的 foundation action models 在專業桌面應用程式方面表現不佳 (約 60% 的任務失敗率)。除了評估之外，CUA-Suite 豐富的多模態語料庫支援新興的研究方向，包括通用螢幕解析、連續空間控制、基於影片的 reward modeling 和 visual world models。所有數據和模型均已公開發布。
top5.title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents
top5.title_zh: CUA-Suite：用於 Computer-Use Agents 的海量人類標註影片示範

links.title: Composer 2 Technical Report

primary_link.title: Composer 2 Technical Report

top5.summary: Composer 2 is a specialized model designed for agentic software engineering. The model demonstrates strong long-term planning and coding intelligence while maintaining the ability to efficiently solve problems for interactive use. top5.summary_zh: Composer 2 是一個專為 agentic 軟體工程設計的專門模型。該模型展現出強大的長期規劃和程式編寫智慧，同時保持了有效解決互動式使用問題的能力。該模型分兩個階段進行訓練：首先是持續的 pretraining，以提高模型的知識和潛在的程式編寫能力；其次是大規模的 reinforcement learning，透過更強大的推理、精確的多步驟執行來提高端到端程式編寫性能。
